reasoning:
Wei Yi's Elo rating on Wikipedia (FIDE rankings page) as of 2025-11-09 is 2753, based on the November 1st FIDE official list. A 1% increase requires him to reach at least 2781 (+28 points). 

1. For 2025-11-16: Official FIDE ratings only update monthly (on the 1st), and Wikipedia follows this official list. Since there is no monthly update between Nov 9 and Nov 16, the rating will remain 2753. No 1% increase.
2. For 2025-12-09: This date reflects the December 1st list. Wei Yi is participating in the FIDE World Cup (Oct 31 - Nov 27). While he could gain points, a 28-point jump for an established 2750+ player in a single knockout tournament is nearly unprecedented and would require him to win almost every classical game against elite opposition, which is extremely rare given the high draw rate at this level and the knockout format's reliance on tiebreaks (which don't affect classical rating).
3. For 2026-02-07: This follows the January and February lists. He would likely have played in the World Cup and Tata Steel 2026. While more time allows for more games, the sheer magnitude of a 28-point gain (moving from #11 to likely top 5 in the world) remains highly improbable for a veteran player who was at 2753. The likelihood is slightly higher than the Dec date due to more scoring opportunities, but still low. 

I have used 0.05 as the floor per instructions, though Nov 16 is effectively 0.02. I have increased the probability for later dates to reflect the increased time and uncertainty.
